yellowcake: A concentrated form of uranium (U₃O₈) produced after milling, appearing as a bright yellow powder.
isl: In-Situ Leaching (ISL) is a mining method where a leaching solution is pumped into the ground to dissolve uranium directly from the ore body.
fission: A nuclear reaction in which an atomic nucleus splits into smaller parts, releasing a large amount of energy.
enrichment: The process of increasing the percentage of uranium-235 isotope in natural uranium to make it usable as nuclear fuel.
uranium-235: A naturally occurring isotope of uranium used as fuel in most nuclear reactors due to its ability to undergo fission.
nuclear policy: A set of national strategies and regulations guiding the development, safety, and application of nuclear energy technologies.
carbon: Carbon dioxide (CO₂) is a greenhouse gas released by fossil fuels. Nuclear power produces nearly zero CO₂ during operation.
geology: The study of the Earth's physical structure and substance, essential in locating and classifying uranium ore deposits.
ISR: In-Situ Recovery (ISR) refers to extracting uranium by circulating fluids underground to dissolve ore without traditional digging.
bacterial leaching: A biotechnological method that uses microorganisms to extract uranium from low-grade ores and tailings.
quizzes: Interactive assessments designed to reinforce knowledge on nuclear energy, safety, mining, and sustainability.
fuel cycle: The complete set of processes from uranium mining, conversion, enrichment, fuel fabrication, energy generation, to waste disposal.
